FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class SelectionModel

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

Index/RANGE-based selection state (E11-L3) — WinUI's SelectionModel flattened to one level (our items controls are flat projections; TreeView flattens before selecting). DECOUPLED FROM REALIZATION: selection is a sorted list of disjoint inclusive index ranges, so SelectAll() over 50k rows stores ONE range and realizes nothing — realized items read their state on prepare via IsSelected(int) (O(log ranges)).

Signals-first: every mutation bumps Version; a composing control reads Version.Value in its render so a selection change re-renders exactly that control (which re-skins only its realized window). SelectionChanged fires once per mutating call that actually changed state (WinUI SelectionChanged).

Pointer/keyboard semantics are the verified WinUI selector trio (controls\dev\ItemsView): OnInteractedAction(int, bool, bool) / OnFocusedAction(int, bool, bool) implement SingleSelector.cpp:25-57, MultipleSelector.cpp:18-92 and ExtendedSelector.cpp:18-83 — anchor+shift ranges, ctrl toggles, focus-follow.

public sealed class SelectionModel

object

Fields

SelectionChanged

Fired once per mutating call that changed the selection (WinUI SelectionModel.SelectionChanged).

public Action? SelectionChanged

Field Value

Action

Properties

AnchorIndex

The shift-range anchor (WinUI SelectionModel.AnchorIndex). −1 = none. Single item ops move it.

public int AnchorIndex { get; set; }

Property Value

int

FirstSelectedIndex 

public int FirstSelectedIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

ItemCount

The collection size selection indices are clamped to. Shrinking trims out-of-range selection.

public int ItemCount { get; set; }

Property Value

int

LastSelectedIndex 

public int LastSelectedIndex { get; }

Property Value

int

Mode

Selection mode. Coercing to Single keeps only the first selected item; None leaves existing (programmatic) selection readable but routes interactions to a null selector.

public ItemsSelectionMode Mode { get; set; }

Property Value

ItemsSelectionMode

RangeCount 

public int RangeCount { get; }

Property Value

int

SelectedCount

Total selected items (sum over ranges, O(ranges) — never walks indices).

public int SelectedCount { get; }

Property Value

int

SingleSelectionFollowsFocus

In Single mode, moving keyboard focus without Ctrl selects the focused item (WinUI SingleSelector.h m_followFocus, default true).

public bool SingleSelectionFollowsFocus { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

Version

Bumped on every actual change — read it in a render to subscribe that component (signals-first).

public Signal<int> Version { get; }

Property Value

Signal<int>

DeselectRangeFromAnchorTo(int)

WinUI DeselectRangeFromAnchorTo: deselect [anchor, index]; anchor stays.

index int

GetRange(int)

The i-th selected range (inclusive) — for tests/serialization without realizing indices.

public (int Start, int End) GetRange(int i)

Parameters

i int

Returns

(int Start, int End)

GetSelectedIndices(List<int>)

Append every selected index into into (cleared first). The WinUI SelectedItems projection — call only when a materialized list is genuinely needed.

public void GetSelectedIndices(List<int> into)

Parameters

into List<int>

InvertSelection()

Complement over [0, ItemCount): the gaps become the ranges — O(ranges), realizes nothing.

public void InvertSelection()

index int

Returns

bool

Keyboard focus moved to item index (arrows/Home/End/typeahead).

index int
ctrl bool
shift bool

OnInteractedAction(int, bool, bool)

Pointer tap / Space / Enter on item index with the live modifier chord.

public void OnInteractedAction(int index, bool ctrl, bool shift)

Parameters

index int
ctrl bool
shift bool

RemapMove(int, int)

Remap selected indices after a collection RemoveAt(from) + Insert(to) reorder, preserving selected ITEM identity rather than the old slot. Cold drop path.

public void RemapMove(int from, int to)

Parameters

from int
to int

Select(int)

Select one item. In Single mode this REPLACES the selection (WinUI SingleSelect).

public void Select(int index)

Parameters

index int

SelectAll()

ONE stored range regardless of count — select-all over 50k realizes nothing.

public void SelectAll()

SelectRange(int, int)

Select an inclusive index range (clamped). Stores RANGES — O(ranges), realizes nothing.

public void SelectRange(int start, int end)

Parameters

start int
end int

SelectRangeFromAnchorTo(int)

WinUI SelectRangeFromAnchorTo: select [anchor, index] (order-normalized); anchor stays.

public void SelectRangeFromAnchorTo(int index)

Parameters

index int

Parameters

index int
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
